schemaVersion: Ehel English Course Final Assessment v1.2
subject: English
title: Grade 5 English Final Course Quiz
description: A cumulative assessment of vocabulary, reading, language and communication across Units 1-9.

sections.title: Words and Meaning
sections.description: Use Grade 5 vocabulary accurately in familiar and new contexts.

sections.title: Reading and Language
sections.description: Show reading understanding and control of Grade 5 language patterns.

sections.title: Communication and Application
sections.description: Apply English to speaking, writing and purposeful communication.

questions.quizTitle: There’s a Lesson in That checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'coal' mean?
questions.options: A black rock burned for heat and energy | Areas or parts of a country with something in common | To meet or come across someone by chance | When the air has tiny water drops, making it hard to see
questions.correctAnswer: A black rock burned for heat and energy
questions.explanation: We use coal to help make electricity for our homes.

questions.quizTitle: There’s a Lesson in That checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'pouch' mean?
questions.options: A feeling of worry or nervousness | A small bag or pocket that can hold things | Areas or parts of a country with something in common | When the air has tiny water drops, making it hard to see
questions.correctAnswer: A small bag or pocket that can hold things
questions.explanation: He kept his coins in a small leather pouch.

questions.quizTitle: There’s a Lesson in That checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'encounter' mean?
questions.options: A feeling of worry or nervousness | Related to the sea or ocean | To meet or come across someone by chance | When the air has tiny water drops, making it hard to see
questions.correctAnswer: To meet or come across someone by chance
questions.explanation: She had an unexpected encounter with an old friend at the market.

questions.quizTitle: There’s a Lesson in That checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'regions' mean?
questions.options: A feeling of worry or nervousness | A person who was born in a particular place | Areas or parts of a country with something in common | Related to the sea or ocean
questions.correctAnswer: Areas or parts of a country with something in common
questions.explanation: There are many different regions in our country, each with its own culture.

questions.question: What does 'independence' mean?
questions.options: A large area of land that is controlled by one ruler or government | A very bright light that is hard to see in | The ability to take care of yourself and make your own choices | To show something that is usually hidden or to make something known
questions.correctAnswer: The ability to take care of yourself and make your own choices
questions.explanation: On her birthday, she felt proud of her independence because she could choose her own cake.

questions.question: What does 'issue' mean?
questions.options: A place or thing where something comes from | A problem or topic that people talk about or need to think about | A very bright light that is hard to see in | To show something that is usually hidden or to make something known
questions.correctAnswer: A problem or topic that people talk about or need to think about
questions.explanation: We had a big issue with our school project, so we worked together to fix it.

questions.question: What does 'empire' mean?
questions.options: A large area of land that is controlled by one ruler or government | A place or thing where something comes from | A very bright light that is hard to see in | To work together with others to achieve something
questions.correctAnswer: A large area of land that is controlled by one ruler or government
questions.explanation: The Roman Empire was very powerful and included many countries.

questions.question: What does 'expose' mean?
questions.options: A place or thing where something comes from | To show something that is usually hidden or to make something known | To work together with others to achieve something | When people decide not to buy or use something to show they disagree
questions.correctAnswer: To show something that is usually hidden or to make something known
questions.explanation: The teacher will expose the secret to the class during the lesson.

questions.question: What does 'policy' mean?
questions.options: A plan or rule that helps people decide what to do. | A set of rules that explains how a country or organisation works. | A special document that gives rights or rules to a group. | Related to making laws or rules.
questions.correctAnswer: A plan or rule that helps people decide what to do.
questions.explanation: Our school has a policy that everyone must wear a uniform.

questions.question: What does 'lobby' mean?
questions.options: A large area inside a building where people wait or meet. | A special document that gives rights or rules to a group. | A special job or task that someone is trying to do. | Related to making laws or rules.
questions.correctAnswer: A large area inside a building where people wait or meet.
questions.explanation: We waited in the lobby of the museum for our guide.

questions.question: What does 'constitution' mean?
questions.options: A set of rules that explains how a country or organisation works. | A special job or task that someone is trying to do. | Related to making laws or rules. | The special purpose or job that something has.
questions.correctAnswer: A set of rules that explains how a country or organisation works.
questions.explanation: The constitution of our school tells us the rights and rules we follow.

questions.question: What does 'charter' mean?
questions.options: A special document that gives rights or rules to a group. | A special job or task that someone is trying to do. | The process of something getting bigger or growing. | The special purpose or job that something has.
questions.correctAnswer: A special document that gives rights or rules to a group.
questions.explanation: The school received a charter to start a new programme for students.

questions.question: What does 'hasty' mean?
questions.options: Doing something too quickly without thinking carefully. | Feeling happy and thankful for something nice someone has done. | Very angry or mad. | Worried or nervous about something that might happen.
questions.correctAnswer: Doing something too quickly without thinking carefully.
questions.explanation: He made a hasty decision to leave the game early.

questions.question: What does 'reluctant' mean?
questions.options: Easily annoyed or made angry. | Feeling happy and thankful for something nice someone has done. | Not wanting to do something or feeling unsure about it. | Worried or nervous about something that might happen.
questions.correctAnswer: Not wanting to do something or feeling unsure about it.
questions.explanation: She was reluctant to try the new food because she didn't know if she would like it.

questions.question: What does 'furious' mean?
questions.options: Easily annoyed or made angry. | Feeling happy and thankful for something nice someone has done. | Feeling very sad or unhappy. | Very angry or mad.
questions.correctAnswer: Very angry or mad.
questions.explanation: She was furious when her brother broke her favourite toy.

questions.question: What does 'anxious' mean?
questions.options: Easily annoyed or made angry. | Feeling very sad or unhappy. | Nice and enjoyable. | Worried or nervous about something that might happen.
questions.correctAnswer: Worried or nervous about something that might happen.
questions.explanation: I felt anxious before my big test at school.

questions.question: What does 'communicate' mean?
questions.options: To convince someone to do or believe something. | To make someone believe that something is true. | To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly. | To share information or ideas with someone.
questions.correctAnswer: To share information or ideas with someone.
questions.explanation: Good writers communicate their ideas clearly.

questions.question: What does 'quote' mean?
questions.options: A group of words that someone has said or written. | To convince someone to do or believe something. | To make someone believe that something is true. | To support someone or give them confidence.
questions.correctAnswer: A group of words that someone has said or written.
questions.explanation: Sarah shared a quote from her grandmother.

questions.question: What does 'emphasize' mean?
questions.options: To make someone believe that something is true. | To make something seem bigger or more important than it really is. | To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly. | To support someone or give them confidence.
questions.correctAnswer: To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly.
questions.explanation: The teacher will emphasize the key points in the lesson.

questions.question: What does 'persuade' mean?
questions.options: To convince someone to do or believe something. | To make something seem bigger or more important than it really is. | To support someone or give them confidence. | To think something is true without proof.
questions.correctAnswer: To convince someone to do or believe something.
questions.explanation: She tried to persuade her friend to join the team.

questions.quizTitle: A Different Type of Story checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'unrest' mean?
questions.options: A situation where people are unhappy and may protest or cause trouble. | Not achieving the desired result or goal. | Not known or recognised; something you have not seen before. | Something that happens suddenly and is not planned.
questions.correctAnswer: A situation where people are unhappy and may protest or cause trouble.
questions.explanation: There was unrest in the city because people wanted to change the rules.

questions.quizTitle: A Different Type of Story checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'uneasy' mean?
questions.options: Feeling worried or uncomfortable about something. | Not achieving the desired result or goal. | Not known or recognised; something you have not seen before. | Not sure about something.
questions.correctAnswer: Feeling worried or uncomfortable about something.
questions.explanation: I felt uneasy when I heard a strange noise in the dark.

questions.quizTitle: A Different Type of Story checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'unexpected' mean?
questions.options: Not knowing or realising something. | Not known or recognised; something you have not seen before. | Not sure about something. | Something that happens suddenly and is not planned.
questions.correctAnswer: Something that happens suddenly and is not planned.
questions.explanation: The rain was unexpected, so we got wet without our umbrellas.

questions.quizTitle: A Different Type of Story checkpoint
questions.question: What does 'unsuccessful' mean?
questions.options: Not achieving the desired result or goal. | Not awake and not aware of what is happening. | Not knowing or realising something. | Not sure about something.
questions.correctAnswer: Not achieving the desired result or goal.
questions.explanation: He tried to climb the tree, but he was unsuccessful and fell down.

questions.question: What does 'local' mean?
questions.options: A large area of land on Earth surrounded by water. | Having a lot of space; not crowded or small. | Related to a specific place or area near you. | Related to the countryside, not the city.
questions.correctAnswer: Related to a specific place or area near you.
questions.explanation: The local park is where we play every afternoon.

questions.question: What does 'urban' mean?
questions.options: A large area of land on Earth surrounded by water. | Having a lot of space; not crowded or small. | Related to a city or town. | Related to a time when one country controlled another.
questions.correctAnswer: Related to a city or town.
questions.explanation: The urban centre had tall buildings and busy streets.

questions.question: What does 'rural' mean?
questions.options: A large area of land on Earth surrounded by water. | Related to a time when one country controlled another. | Related to events that happened in the past. | Related to the countryside, not the city.
questions.correctAnswer: Related to the countryside, not the city.
questions.explanation: The rural village was peaceful and surrounded by farms.

questions.question: What does 'spacious' mean?
questions.options: Having a lot of space; not crowded or small. | Related to a king or queen. | Related to a time when one country controlled another. | Related to events that happened in the past.
questions.correctAnswer: Having a lot of space; not crowded or small.
questions.explanation: The hall was spacious enough for the whole class.

questions.question: What does 'intelligence' mean?
questions.options: A doctor who helps people stay healthy and treats them when sick. | A person who studies maps and the Earth's features. | The ability to learn, understand, and think about things. | Very old; from a long time ago.
questions.correctAnswer: The ability to learn, understand, and think about things.
questions.explanation: Her intelligence helped her solve the tricky problem.

questions.question: What does 'citizenship' mean?
questions.options: A person who studies maps and the Earth's features. | The status of being a member of a country with rights and responsibilities. | Very beautiful or impressive. | Very old; from a long time ago.
questions.correctAnswer: The status of being a member of a country with rights and responsibilities.
questions.explanation: Citizenship means you can vote and go to school.
